project.name: Atlas Workbench
project.purpose: A modern Linux desktop control panel that wraps the user's existing llama.cpp binary, exposing every server flag, model operation, and fine-tuning parameter through clean widgets instead of command-line memorization.
project.where_it_runs: Linux desktop, installed via .deb or AppImage, launches as a native window wrapping the user's existing llama.cpp server and finetune binaries as child processes

features.name: Server Control Tab
features.description: A tab where the user starts and stops the llama.cpp server, configures host and port, and sees real-time server stdout/stderr in a log panel.
entry_points: Server tab in the main tab layout
requirements: MUST provide a Start button that launches the llama.cpp server binary as a child process with the currently configured flags
MUST provide a Stop button that gracefully terminates the server child process
MUST provide host and port input fields that map to the --host and --port flags
MUST display server stdout and stderr in a scrollable log panel in real time
MUST show server status (running/stopped) as a visible indicator
MUST disable the Start button when the server is already running and disable Stop when it is not
acceptance_criteria: Clicking Start launches the server process and the log panel shows startup output
Clicking Stop terminates the server process and the status indicator changes to stopped
Changing host and port values changes the flags passed to the server binary on next launch
The log panel updates in real time as the server writes to stdout/stderr
verification: Scenario test: configure host=127.0.0.1 port=8080, click Start, assert process is running, assert HTTP response on port 8080, click Stop, assert process is terminated
Scenario test: start server, verify log panel contains expected server startup text within 5 seconds
done_requires: feature_list.json status is done
verification/evidence/server-control.md exists
evidence lists verification commands that were actually run
evidence lists wiring audit from entry point to implementation
evidence lists tests or scenarios proving every acceptance criterion
evidence lists no known gaps

features.name: Model Management Tab
features.description: A tab where the user browses for GGUF model files, loads a model into the server, and unloads it.
entry_points: Models tab in the main tab layout
requirements: MUST provide a file browser or directory picker to select a folder containing GGUF model files
MUST list all .gguf files found in the selected directory
MUST allow the user to select a model file to load
MUST pass the selected model path to the server as the --model flag
MUST show which model is currently loaded
MUST provide an Unload button that clears the current model selection
MUST persist the last-used model directory and selected model between sessions
acceptance_criteria: Browsing to a directory lists all .gguf files in that directory
Selecting a model and clicking Load sets it as the active model for the server
The loaded model name is visible in the UI
Clicking Unload clears the model selection
Closing and reopening the app restores the last model directory and selection
verification: Scenario test: browse to a directory with GGUF files, assert files are listed, select one, click Load, start server, assert server log shows the correct model path
Scenario test: point to a nonexistent directory, assert a clear error message appears
done_requires: feature_list.json status is done
verification/evidence/model-management.md exists
evidence lists verification commands that were actually run
evidence lists wiring audit from entry point to implementation
evidence lists tests or scenarios proving every acceptance criterion
evidence lists no known gaps

features.name: Settings & Flags Tab
features.description: A tab exposing every llama.cpp server flag as a modern widget — dropdowns, sliders, toggles, and text inputs — organized in a clean layout.
entry_points: Settings tab in the main tab layout
Flag widgets (dropdowns, sliders, toggles, text inputs)
requirements: MUST expose every llama.cpp server flag as an appropriate widget (dropdown for enum-like flags, slider for numeric ranges, toggle for booleans, text input for strings)
MUST group flags into logical sections (e.g., Performance, Context, Sampling, GPU/Acceleration)
MUST show the current value of each flag in its widget
MUST pass all configured flag values to the server binary on launch
MUST persist all flag values between sessions
MUST provide a Reset to Defaults button for each section or globally
MUST show a tooltip or help text for each flag explaining what it does in plain language
acceptance_criteria: Every llama.cpp server flag has a corresponding widget
Changing a widget value updates the flag passed to the server on next launch
Flag values persist after closing and reopening the app
Reset to Defaults restores all flags to their default values
Each flag has a tooltip with a plain-language description
verification: Scenario test: change context size to 4096, start server, assert server log shows --ctx-size 4096
Scenario test: enable GPU offload with a slider value of 20, start server, assert server log shows --n-gpu-layers 20
Scenario test: change settings, close app, reopen app, assert all previously set values are restored
done_requires: feature_list.json status is done
verification/evidence/settings-flags.md exists
evidence lists verification commands that were actually run
evidence lists wiring audit from entry point to implementation
evidence lists tests or scenarios proving every acceptance criterion
evidence lists no known gaps

features.name: Fine-Tuning Tab
features.description: A tab where the user configures and runs llama.cpp fine-tuning with form controls for dataset path, learning rate, epochs, output path, and other training parameters.
entry_points: Fine-tuning tab in the main tab layout
requirements: MUST provide a file picker for the training dataset path
MUST provide a file picker or text input for the output model path
MUST provide form controls for learning rate, epochs, batch size, and other finetune binary parameters
MUST launch the llama.cpp finetune binary as a child process with all configured parameters
MUST display training progress and logs in a real-time log panel
MUST show a clear success message when training completes and the output model file exists
MUST show a clear error message if training fails, with plain-language explanation
MUST provide a Stop Training button to terminate the finetune process
MUST persist fine-tuning configuration between sessions
acceptance_criteria: Configuring all parameters and clicking Start Training launches the finetune binary
Training logs appear in real time in the log panel
When training completes, the output model file exists at the specified path
If training fails, a clear error message appears in the UI
Clicking Stop Training terminates the finetune process
Fine-tuning settings persist after closing and reopening the app
verification: Scenario test: configure a small dataset, set 1 epoch, set output path, click Start Training, wait for completion, assert output model file exists at the specified path
Scenario test: point to a nonexistent dataset, click Start Training, assert a clear error message appears
Scenario test: start training, click Stop Training, assert the finetune process is terminated
done_requires: feature_list.json status is done
verification/evidence/fine-tuning.md exists
evidence lists verification commands that were actually run
evidence lists wiring audit from entry point to implementation
evidence lists tests or scenarios proving every acceptance criterion
evidence lists no known gaps

features.name: System Monitor Tab
features.description: A tab showing real-time CPU, RAM, and GPU usage metrics so the user can see exactly what is happening with their system while the server or training runs.
entry_points: System Monitor tab in the main tab layout
requirements: MUST display real-time CPU usage (overall percentage and per-core if available)
MUST display real-time RAM usage (used/total)
MUST display real-time GPU usage if a GPU is detected
MUST update metrics at least once per second without freezing the UI
MUST show a clear 'GPU not detected' message if no GPU is available
MUST show the llama.cpp child process resource usage separately if the server or training is running
acceptance_criteria: CPU and RAM metrics update at least once per second
GPU metrics display when a GPU is present, or a 'GPU not detected' message when not
When the server is running, the llama.cpp process resource usage is visible
The UI does not freeze or stutter while metrics update
verification: Scenario test: open System Monitor tab, assert CPU and RAM values update within 2 seconds, assert no UI freeze
Scenario test: start server, open System Monitor, assert llama.cpp process appears in the process resource display
Scenario test: run on a machine without a GPU, assert 'GPU not detected' message is shown
done_requires: feature_list.json status is done
verification/evidence/system-monitor.md exists
evidence lists verification commands that were actually run
evidence lists wiring audit from entry point to implementation
evidence lists tests or scenarios proving every acceptance criterion
evidence lists no known gaps

features.name: Binary Configuration & First Launch
features.description: On first launch, the app detects whether the llama.cpp binary path is configured. If not, it prompts the user to locate the server and finetune binaries, validates they are executable, and persists the paths.
entry_points: Binary path settings section (accessible from Settings tab or a menu)
requirements: MUST check on launch whether the llama.cpp server binary path is configured and exists
MUST prompt the user with a file picker to locate the server binary if it is not configured
MUST also prompt for the finetune binary path (may be the same binary or a separate one)
MUST validate that the selected files exist and are executable before accepting them
MUST persist the binary paths in a config file between sessions
MUST show a clear error message if the binary is not executable or does not exist
MUST allow the user to change the binary path later from a Settings section
acceptance_criteria: On first launch with no config, the app prompts for the server binary path
Selecting a valid executable path is accepted and persisted
Selecting a nonexistent or non-executable file shows a clear error and re-prompts
Closing and reopening the app does not re-prompt if valid paths were previously saved
The user can change the binary path from the settings section at any time
verification: Scenario test: delete config file, launch app, assert binary path prompt appears, provide valid path, assert prompt does not reappear on next launch
Scenario test: provide a nonexistent file path, assert error message appears and prompt re-shows
Scenario test: provide a non-executable file, assert error message appears
done_requires: feature_list.json status is done
verification/evidence/binary-config.md exists
evidence lists verification commands that were actually run
evidence lists wiring audit from entry point to implementation
evidence lists tests or scenarios proving every acceptance criterion
evidence lists no known gaps

features.name: Error Handling & Recovery
features.description: A cross-cutting feature ensuring that every process failure, missing file, invalid configuration, or crash surfaces as a clear in-app error message in plain language, allowing the user to fix the issue and retry.
entry_points: Error message dialog or banner (appears in any tab when an error occurs)
Retry button in the error message
Dismiss button in the error message
requirements: MUST show a visible in-app error message (not a terminal or console log) for every failure condition
MUST write error messages in plain language that explains what went wrong and suggests a fix
MUST not silently swallow errors or hide them in logs only
MUST allow the user to dismiss the error and retry the operation
MUST log errors to an internal log file for diagnostics in addition to showing them in the UI
MUST handle child process crashes by showing an error message with the process exit code and last stderr output
acceptance_criteria: When the server binary crashes, an error message appears with the exit code and relevant stderr output
When a model file does not exist, an error message appears explaining the file was not found
When fine-tuning fails, an error message appears explaining the failure reason
Every error message includes a plain-language explanation and a suggested fix
The user can dismiss the error and retry the operation
verification: Scenario test: point to a nonexistent model file, click Start, assert error message appears with 'file not found' explanation
Scenario test: kill the server process externally, assert the app shows an error message with the exit code
Scenario test: start fine-tuning with an invalid dataset, assert error message appears with explanation
done_requires: feature_list.json status is done
verification/evidence/error-handling.md exists
evidence lists verification commands that were actually run
evidence lists wiring audit from entry point to implementation
evidence lists tests or scenarios proving every acceptance criterion
evidence lists no known gaps
